operating_state: Net sales increased $21.9 billion to $132.1 billion driven by 19.7% comparable sales growth, reflecting 10.5% higher average ticket and 8.6% more customer transactions. Online sales grew 86% as COVID-19 accelerated digital adoption across the One Home Depot interconnected retail strategy. Eleven of fourteen merchandising departments posted double-digit comparable sales growth with broad-based strength across core categories.
cost_structure: Gross profit margin compressed 10 basis points to 34.0% due to product mix shifts, higher shrink, and increased supply chain expenses, partially offset by reduced promotional activity and expanding proprietary products. SG&A increased 480 basis points to 18.5% of sales driven by enhanced associate pay and benefits, COVID-19 operational expenses, and higher success sharing program costs reflecting competitive labor market conditions.
material_events: Completed HD Supply acquisition for $7.8 billion net consideration in December 2020 to accelerate MRO market penetration. Issued $8.0 billion in long-term debt while repaying $2.9 billion of existing debt. Suspended share repurchases in March 2020 and resumed in first quarter fiscal 2021, with announced 10% quarterly dividend increase to $1.65 per share in February 2021.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 pandemic created supply chain disruptions and inventory availability pressures while driving significant acceleration in customer demand for home improvement projects as spending potentially shifts back to travel and entertainment. Stronger U.S. dollar negatively impacted sales growth by $381 million while commodity price inflation, primarily lumber, contributed to average ticket increases and affects both product costs and customer demand patterns.
forward_outlook: Management expects to complete deferred in-store strategic investments in fiscal 2021 with capital expenditures estimated at approximately 2% of net sales annually while continuing investments in One Supply Chain infrastructure and One Home Depot experience capabilities. Company intends to maintain elevated cash position during fiscal 2021 to enhance liquidity with share repurchase continuation influenced by evolving economic and pandemic environment conditions.
uncertainty_profile: HD Supply integration execution and realization of anticipated MRO market synergies present operational complexity around systems consolidation. Ongoing COVID-19 impacts on supply chain operations and customer demand patterns remain unpredictable with potential for additional governmental restrictions. Cybersecurity threats have intensified with increased digital transaction volumes while EPA consent decree from installation services oversight creates ongoing regulatory compliance requirements.
